Contract editing work

Freelance Video Editor Jobs

Freelance video editing is strongest when the brief is specific: content format, assets, edit length, revision process, rate, delivery cadence, and examples of the desired pacing.

A clean freelance category helps editors compare real work instead of sorting through vague gig posts.

Join editor alerts Post a role early

Role examples

Early categories before live listings

Remote

Short-form video editor

Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, creator teams

  • fast turnaround
  • caption styling
  • social hooks
Remote or hybrid

YouTube channel editor

Long-form YouTube, podcasts, education, creator-led brands

  • story pacing
  • retention edits
  • thumbnail collaboration
Contract

Brand video editor

B2B, ecommerce, product launches, paid social

  • motion basics
  • ad variants
  • stakeholder review
Entry level

Assistant editor

Post houses, agencies, production teams

  • media management
  • proxy workflows
  • Premiere or Resolve

Freelance signals that matter

Useful listings should state rate structure, source footage volume, number of deliverables, revision rounds, deadline expectations, music/licensing responsibility, and whether motion graphics are required.

Good matches for freelance editors

Creator channels, event recap teams, ecommerce brands, podcast networks, course creators, newsletters, and agencies often hire freelance editors before they hire full-time.

Hiring teams

Post a video editor role early

Good listings should include content format, edit volume, software stack, compensation, timezone or location, revision cadence, and portfolio examples.

Connect this form to TalentPrism when the backend posting flow is ready.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

Should freelance editor jobs list hourly or project rates?

Either can work, but listings should make the scope clear. Project rates need deliverable counts and revision expectations; hourly roles need an expected weekly range.

What portfolio should a freelance video editor share?

Share clips that match the job's format. A YouTube role needs pacing and retention examples; paid social needs variant and hook examples; brand work needs polish and review discipline.
